data.title: Summarizing compaction for old closed tickets
data.description:
## Context

The DSO ticket system appends events indefinitely. A long-lived ticket with dozens of transitions, comments, links, and bridge syncs accumulates a large directory that slows reducer replay and bloats the `tickets` branch disk footprint. The `.claude/scripts/dso ticket compact` command exists today for single-ticket compaction but is not routinely applied, has no automatic trigger, and loses comment body detail when it snapshots. The beads project applies summarizing compaction to old closed tickets — collapsing the full history into a concise summary. This epic introduces analogous automatic policy-driven compaction for DSO while preserving full audit integrity via the REVERT mechanism and bridge event preservation.

## Success Criteria
- Compaction policy: closed tickets older than N days (configurable, default 90) are eligible. Archived tickets eligible immediately.
- `ticket compact --all --dry-run` lists eligible tickets and projected space savings without writing any events.
- `ticket compact --all` runs policy-driven compaction producing a single SNAPSHOT event plus a summary COMMENT that records: final status, resolution value, closing reason, number of comments/links replaced, and a brief LLM-generated summary of the ticket journey.
- Bridge-origin events (SYNC, BRIDGE_ALERT) are NEVER compacted — they remain queryable even after compaction of surrounding events.
- Compaction is reversible: the compaction event itself is logged so `ticket-revert.sh` can undo a specific compaction if needed.
- Scheduled maintenance mode integrates with existing `/dso:end-session` or a new periodic hook; running `--all` completes within 5 minutes for a repo with 2,000+ tickets.

## Approach
Extend `ticket-compact.sh` with an `--all` flag that iterates over net-eligible tickets (closed + age >= N days, excluding archived-only tickets with existing snapshots). Each compaction writes one SNAPSHOT event capturing compiled state plus a summary COMMENT — this preserves searchability by `ticket show`. Use the existing SNAPSHOT event format; no new event types needed. LLM summary generation is delegated to a lightweight prompt via the haiku model; if unavailable, fall back to a structured deterministic summary (no LLM).

## Dependencies
No upstream dependencies. Independent housekeeping epic.

## Out of Scope
- Full tickets-branch git GC (separate concern)
- Deletion of tickets (tombstoning is Epic 6f7a-4ba6 scope)
- Remote-side Jira history compaction
